message:
fix(widgets, extras): cursor safety, toast lifecycle, tree lookup, scroll, and chart labels

TextInput: Cursor positioning after insert_str and insert_char now uses
byte-offset-based grapheme counting instead of delta arithmetic, which
broke when combining characters merged with preceding graphemes. All
deletion methods (delete_char_back, delete_char_forward, delete_word_*,
delete_selection, undo) now clamp cursor to grapheme_count() after
mutation, preventing out-of-bounds panics when grapheme merging reduces
the total count.

NotificationQueue + Toast: The toast expiry lifecycle was incorrect —
toasts were removed from the visible list before their dismiss animation
could play. Now, expired toasts are explicitly dismissed first (which
triggers the exit animation), then tick_animation advances state, and
only toasts that reach the Hidden phase are removed. The is_visible()
check is simplified to only inspect animation phase, since dismissed and
expired toasts still need to be visible during their exit animation.
Tests updated to use no_animation() to preserve synchronous behavior.

Tree: node_at_visible_index_mut used string-based path matching to
navigate from root to the target node. This failed when sibling nodes
shared the same label, as it would always match the first occurrence.
Replaced with index-based path tracking that records child indices
during traversal, making the lookup O(depth) and unambiguous.

VirtualizedList: Scrollbar visibility and scroll offset clamping used
items_per_viewport (computed with div_ceil for partial items), which
showed the scrollbar prematurely and allowed scrolling past the end.
Now uses fully_visible_items (floor division) so the scrollbar only
appears when items genuinely overflow, and the selected item is kept
fully visible rather than partially clipped.

BarChart (extras): Horizontal bar labels were truncated to a single
grapheme with a hardcoded 2-column label area. Now measures the longest
label dynamically (capped at 1/3 of chart width) and renders full label
text. Also ensures non-zero values always render at least 1 character
of bar length, and zero values render no bar.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
